# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
Started: (will be replaced on first run)
---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).
- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
- **US-010 hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055:** the cascade trigger (`app_private.bump_listing_version`) swaps `listings.current_version_id` to the new version, but it does NOT refresh the cached `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` threshold columns. Those columns are populated by §15b (`app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds` on `listing_conditions` + `app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump` on `listings.current_version_id`) which was intentionally scoped OUT of US-010 per its AC. Before any edge function that calls `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` ships, or the discover feed's eligibility filter (US-038) is wired up, §15b triggers MUST land — otherwise threshold columns silently go stale on every conditions-only bump, the feed's threshold index returns wrong eligibility, and the filter lies. Which story claims §15b is open (earliest candidate US-038; the campaign-creation wizard US-049-055 is the latest safe slot).
- **`pg_net.net.http_post(url := NULL, ...)` is a silent no-op, not an error.** When the URL or any required string is NULL (e.g. a `vault.decrypted_secrets` lookup misses), pg_net returns a request id without raising and `cron.job_run_details` logs the run as `succeeded`. Always wrap pg_net calls inside cron bodies in a PL/pgSQL `do $body$ ... $body$` block that resolves required arguments first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` when any are NULL — that surfaces the misconfiguration in `cron.job_run_details` instead of pretending the job ran. Pattern documented in `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql`.
- **Vault-secret pattern for cron-invoked edge functions:** secrets live in `vault.decrypted_secrets` (named `marketify_<purpose>` so they cluster in the dashboard). Migrations DO NOT create the secrets — the actual key material is set out-of-band via the Supabase dashboard. Migrations document the required secret names in the header comment so the next operator knows what to populate. Pre-population, the cron raises and the failure is visible in `cron.job_run_details`. Required secrets so far: `marketify_project_url`, `marketify_service_role_key` (both for `cron-refresh-metrics`).
- **`pg_cron` job-name idempotency requires a guarded `cron.unschedule`.** `cron.schedule` raises on duplicate jobname; `cron.unschedule(text)` raises when the name is unknown. Always wrap in `do $$ begin if exists (select 1 from cron.job where jobname = '<name>') then perform cron.unschedule('<name>'); end if; end $$;` before re-scheduling. There is no upsert in the pg_cron 1.6 API.
- **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).
- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.
- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.
- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.
- **Atomic multi-insert edge-function flow uses a PL/pgSQL RPC, not back-to-back supabase-js inserts.** Two `supabase.rpc`/`.insert()` calls are two separate PostgREST transactions, so a mid-flight crash leaves orphan rows. The established pattern (US-014) is a single `public.<flow>_rpc` PL/pgSQL function invoked via `supabase.rpc(...)` from the edge function — PL/pgSQL wraps the body in an implicit transaction. Catch `unique_violation` with `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint = constraint_name` and return `{error: CODE}` jsonb; let everything else `RAISE` so the edge function can log+500. Implicit-constraint names (`<table>_<column>_key`) are Postgres defaults — ALWAYS comment the expected names in the exception block, since a future migration that renames them would silently downgrade 409s to 500s. Revoke execute from anon/authenticated/public; grant only to service_role (no SECURITY DEFINER needed — service_role already has BYPASSRLS).
- **Supabase edge-function secrets are set via `supabase secrets set --env-file <tmpfile> --project-ref <ref>`, not via Supabase MCP.** The MCP has no secrets tool. Create a temp file containing `NAME=value`, run the CLI, delete the temp file. Never echo the secret value into chat. After setting, re-invoke the edge function — secret names are available via `Deno.env.get(...)`. Running the edge function before the secret is set surfaces as a 500 at the point the helper tries to read it (for `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, that's `signJwt` → `"MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET is not set"` → caught → returned as `{error: "JWT_ERROR"}` in the current auth-* pattern).
- **Edge-function smoke tests live under `scripts/test/<fn-name>.ts`, invoked with `bun run`.** Read `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from `process.env` (bun auto-loads `.env`). Send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers even for `verify_jwt=false` functions — the Supabase gateway still requires a valid apikey at the edge. Use `Date.now()` for unique test fixture values so repeat runs don't collide. Every conflict branch (e.g. 409 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 409 EMAIL_TAKEN) gets its own case — the constraint-name mapping in the RPC's exception handler is only validated by cases that actually hit each branch. Clean up test rows via `execute_sql` after each run.
- **Anonymous `unique` declarations in CREATE TABLE get Postgres default names (`<table>_<column>_key`)** — e.g. `username citext not null unique` on `public.users` produces `users_username_key`. Any PL/pgSQL that inspects `constraint_name` depends on this. Verify via `select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'public.<table>'::regclass` before hardcoding a name. If you later want to rename, drop+re-add with explicit `constraint <name> unique (col)` — and update every dependent exception handler in the same migration.
- **Apify REST endpoint + slug encoding:** use `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<rest-slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_KEY>&waitForFinish=<secs>`. REST slugs replace `/` with `~` (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`). `waitForFinish` makes Apify block up to N seconds before returning the run object — if the run exceeds N, Apify returns `status=RUNNING` and webhooks pick up the terminal state later. Response shape is `{ data: { id, defaultDatasetId, status, ... } }`. For this project the wrapper at `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` uses direct REST (not the `apify-client` SDK) because Deno in Edge Runtime handles plain fetch cleanly and `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` is the sanctioned way to read the token.
- **Never echo external API error bodies into thrown Error strings when the request URL carries a secret in the query string.** Apify (and several other providers) sometimes include the request URL in error payloads, so `throw new Error(\`... ${body}\`)` can land the token in edge-function logs. Throw a fixed-string error with status + slug only; if you need the body for debugging, log it separately with redaction.
- **Deno tests for `_shared/` modules live alongside them as `<name>.test.ts` and run with `deno test --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/<name>.test.ts`.** Assertions come from `jsr:@std/assert@1`. Mock fetch by replacing `globalThis.fetch` with a spy that records `{url, init}` and returns a canned `Response`; always restore in a `try/finally`. Deno isn't on every dev machine; `brew install deno` on macOS is a one-liner. If Deno isn't available in a given iteration, document it in the progress entry and have the CI for this run the tests.
- **Public async wrappers must be declared `async` if they do any synchronous validation that might throw**, so the throw surfaces as a Promise rejection rather than a synchronous exception. `assertRejects` in `@std/assert` fails with "Function throws when expected to reject" if the function throws synchronously — a real footgun that can only be caught by actually running the test.
- **Constant-time dual-secret compare for rotation-aware webhook auth:** edge functions that authenticate third-party webhooks via a shared secret must (a) compare in constant time (XOR-accumulate over `max(len(a), len(b))` bytes, iterate the longer one), (b) check BOTH `<SECRET>` and `<SECRET>_PREVIOUS` env vars without short-circuit, and (c) return false if neither env var is set. The second point is subtle: `return timingSafeEqual(a, current) || timingSafeEqual(a, previous)` short-circuits when the first matches, which means an attacker who has recovered `current` can time-distinguish whether `previous` is configured. Correct shape is `let matchA = current ? timingSafeEqual(provided, current) : false; let matchB = previous ? timingSafeEqual(provided, previous) : false; return matchA || matchB` — both evaluated unconditionally. Reference: `supabase/functions/apify-webhook/index.ts` `verifyWebhookSecret`.
- **Third-party-webhook-receiver edge functions deploy with `verify_jwt=false`** (Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function({verify_jwt: false})`). The provider cannot send an `Authorization: Bearer <marketify-jwt>` — authentication is via the provider's own header (e.g. `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret`). The Supabase API gateway still requires a valid project `apikey` though, so smoke tests must send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers in addition to the webhook-specific auth header. For Apify specifically, pair this with a `payloadTemplate` in the per-run webhook registration that injects `social_link_id` / `scrape_mode` / `run_id` / `apify_finished_at` — these fields are NOT in Apify's default event shape and are required by our receiver contract.
- **Webhook-receiver idempotency via service-role RPC + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING:** the receiver's persistence step goes through a PL/pgSQL RPC that `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (<run-id-col>) WHERE <run-id-col> IS NOT NULL DO NOTHING` and returns jsonb `{inserted: bool, duplicate: bool, <id>?: uuid}`. Paired with a partial unique index on the run-id column, replays of the same webhook are safe. Revoke the RPC from anon/authenticated/public and grant only to service_role. The RPC is invoked by the edge function with a service-role Supabase client — NO `SECURITY DEFINER` needed because service_role already has BYPASSRLS. Reference: `public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile` + `metric_snapshots_run_uniq` (US-006/016).
- **Scope env-var reads to the branch that actually needs them.** A single top-of-handler `Deno.env.get("OPTIONAL_KEY")` guard that 500s on missing keys will trip branches that don't need the key. For the apify-webhook, `APIFY_KEY` is only needed to fetch the dataset on the SUCCEEDED branch — gating the whole handler on it broke the FAILED/TIMED_OUT/ABORTED branches (which have no dataset). Move the lookup inside the conditional, and only return `SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` when you actually can't proceed.
- **`supabase/functions/<name>-live.ts` convention for Apify-touching e2e tests:** any smoke test that spends real Apify credits lives alongside the cheap-path smoke as `<fn>-live.ts`, is NOT part of the default test suite, and documents what it costs + what external state it touches. US-016 used `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` against `@tiktok` (cheap + stable + public) with seed `social_link_id = 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111020` — good reference fixture for future Apify-touching stories. DB-side verification (snapshot row + denorm trigger) is done separately via MCP `execute_sql` after the script completes.
- **Apify `payloadTemplate` requires `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` for quoted `"{{placeholder}}"` substitution.** Without this flag, quoted placeholders stay literal (`"{{resource.id}}"` delivered as-is) and the receiver's validation layer 400s. Bare unquoted `{{var}}` substitutes raw JSON but breaks `JSON.stringify(payloadTemplate)` because the placeholder isn't valid JSON. Rule: every webhook spec we build has `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` and uses quoted placeholders exclusively. Reference: `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` `ApifyWebhookSpec` + `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`.
- **Apify transitional run statuses are READY, RUNNING, TIMING-OUT, ABORTING.** When `waitForFinish` expires before a run reaches a terminal state, Apify returns one of these four — in all four cases the run continues and the registered webhook will still fire the terminal event later. Map all four to 'refreshing' in any caller that derives a metrics-status from the immediate actor-run response; only `SUCCEEDED` should map to 'fresh' and any other (FAILED/TIMED-OUT/ABORTED) to 'failed'. Don't special-case only RUNNING.
- **Reanimated reduced-motion pattern (callers + helpers):** `withReducedMotion(config, reduced)` in `src/design/motion.ts` spreads `reduceMotion: ReduceMotion.Always` onto any Reanimated `SpringConfig`/`TimingConfig` when the caller's `useReducedMotion()` is true. Reanimated v4 collapses `Always`-flagged animations to an instant snap on `onStart` — this is the sanctioned no-op; do NOT roll your own `withTiming(target, {duration: 0})` wrapper. Call shape: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))` — the **explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic is mandatory** because the presets are declared `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` which narrows them to readonly literal types; under `strict` TS those literals don't satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint without widening. Inline the helper inside `withSpring` rather than caching to a `const spring` — `reduced` can flip at runtime when the user toggles OS reduce-motion. `useReducedMotion()` in `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` uses a module-scope `cached: boolean | null` so only the first hook mount per app session pays the async `AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled()` race — subsequent mounts read the cached value synchronously and never flash `false` on devices with reduce-motion on.
- **Animatable iOS shadow props in `useAnimatedStyle`: `shadowOpacity` + `shadowRadius` only — NOT `shadowOffset`.** `shadowOffset` is a compound `{width, height}` object prop that Reanimated 4 does not interpolate through `useAnimatedStyle`; the underlying UIKit shadow stays at its `StyleSheet` value and the offset never moves. For the neubrutalist "shadow collapses to (0,0) on press" spec (docs/design.md §1.5 line 118), the sanctioned pattern is to **animate `shadowOpacity` from 1 → 0** while simultaneously translating the view `(0,0) → (3,3)` — the shadow visually disappears into the pressed-down button. Reference: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`.
- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.

## 2026-04-17 17:45 — US-013
- Added `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 sign/verify via Web Crypto) and `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (shared `corsHeaders` const). JWT claim shape is `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 though the story AC listed only 4 claims; spec wins.
- `verifyJwt` rejects non-HS256 `alg`, missing claims, `exp ≤ now`, and `iat > now + 60s`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.

42	  xxxl: 48,
43	} as const;
44	
45	export const radii = {
46	  card: 12,
47	  button: 10,
48	  input: 10,
49	  image: 8,
50	  pill: 999,
51	} as const;
52	
53	// Neubrutalist hard shadow — solid ink offset (3,3), opacity 1, radius 0.
54	// RN android uses `elevation`; the hard-shadow look is iOS-primary, android gets a matching elevation hint.
55	export const shadows = {
56	  hard: {
57	    shadowColor: colors.ink,
58	    shadowOffset: { width: 3, height: 3 },
59	    shadowOpacity: 1,
60	    shadowRadius: 0,
61	    elevation: 4,
62	  },
63	  none: {
64	    shadowColor: 'transparent',
65	    shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: 0 },
66	    shadowOpacity: 0,
67	    shadowRadius: 0,
68	    elevation: 0,
69	  },
70	} as const;
71	
72	export type ColorToken = keyof typeof colors;
73	export type SpacingToken = keyof typeof spacing;
74	export type RadiusToken = keyof typeof radii;
75	export type ShadowToken = keyof typeof shadows;

content.content:
19-**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
20-- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
21-- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6–8px.
22:- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills — not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
23-- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.
24-
25-### 1.2 Color Palette
--
29-| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30-|---|---|---|---|
31-| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32:| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33-| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34-| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35-| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
--
40-| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
41-| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
42-| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
43:| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
44-| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
45:| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
46-| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
47:| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
48-| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
49:| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
50-| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |
51-
52-**Contrast targets (measured against `--canvas` `#FFF9F2`):**
--
68-| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
69-| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
70-| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
71:| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
72-| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |
73-
74-**Usage rules:**
--
77-- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
78-- Line-length: max 65 characters → content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.
79-
80:[Omitted long matching line]
81-
82-### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)
83-
84-**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
85-```
86:soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
87:snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
88-bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
89-```
90-
--
98-**Motion rules:**
99-1. **Screen transitions** — stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
100-2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.
101:[Omitted long matching line]
102-4. **Pull-to-refresh on metrics** — custom refresh control. Vertical "pull" reveals a Clash Display counter that rolls up (`withTiming` + numerical interpolation). On success, new value "slams" into place with bouncy spring and `✓` fades in. Delta shows `+1.2K` in success green for 1.5s.
103-5. **Card interactions (CampaignCard)** — on press, the card drops its hard shadow (translate `3px,3px` → `0,0`) and moves `translateX/Y(2px,2px)` to simulate a physical press. Spring.soft.
104-6. **Eligibility check animation** — tap "Apply" → criteria animate in a staggered list (each row fades + slides 8px from left, 60ms stagger). Passing conditions show a green check drawing in (SVG stroke-dashoffset, 280ms). Failing conditions shake once and fade to muted.
--
113-
114-| Attribute | Decision |
115-|---|---|
116:| Corners | Cards 12px, buttons 10px, pills 999px, inputs 10px, images 8px. |
117:| Borders | 2px solid `--ink` on every card, button, input, chip. |
118-| Shadow | `offset (3, 3), color: --ink, opacity: 1, radius: 0` — signature hard shadow. Pressed collapses to (0,0). |
119-| Elevation | Two tiers: "flat" (no shadow) and "lifted" (hard shadow). No z-stacking beyond modal overlay. |
120-| Iconography | **Lucide React Native** exclusively. 24×24 viewBox, 2px stroke. Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants — Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |
--
135-| Welcome | `/welcome` | S | First-launch brand intro | "I'm a Creator" / "I'm a Company" |
136-| Role Select | `/onboarding/role` | S | Commit to role | "Continue" |
137-| Enter Handles (Creator) | `/onboarding/creator-handles` | C | Username + TikTok handle and/or Instagram handle (at least one) | "Continue" |
138:| Fetching Metrics | `/onboarding/metrics` | C | Skeleton while Apify scrapes the profile(s); resolves in ~15–60s or slides into the next screen with a "still working in the background" chip | (auto) |
139-| Company Profile | `/onboarding/company-profile` | L | Capture username, email, org name, logo | "Finish" |
140-
141-*(Note: creator onboarding is handle-based — no OAuth, no platform consent screens. Our Apify scrapers pull public profile data from the handle alone. Handles are unverified in v1; there is no "claim your account" flow. The app simply trusts the creator to enter their own handle.)*
--
313-- **All three fresh** → 220ms soft fade → push `/(creator)/feed`. Success toast: "You're in. N campaigns match you."
314-- **All fresh except one photo-only IG `ig_posts` returned 0 videos** (not a scrape error — a real "this creator has no videos" signal, distinguishable in the webhook's `raw_payload`) → same as above; the creator enters the feed with `instagram_avg_views_last_10 = null` and only sees campaigns that don't require that metric. Success toast: "You're in. Video-views stats were skipped — you have no IG videos."
315-- **At least one `failed`** → do NOT auto-advance. The "Continue in bg" CTA upgrades to "Skip for now — you can add this later" and routes to the feed anyway; the failed handle's profile page later shows a retry card (§5.4). Success criterion is "at least one scrape landed" — if *all three* fail, the screen flips to a full-error state (below).
316:- **"Continue in background"** at any time → immediate route to the feed; outstanding runs complete in the background and update Profile metrics silently. A small `MetricStaleIndicator state='refreshing'` chip appears in the Profile tab's icon dot until all runs land.
317-
318-**"Keep me here"** anchors the screen; useful for impatient first-timers who want to watch the rows flip. No timeout — if the user stays, they stay.
319-
--
353-│ │ 🔍  Search campaigns               │    │
354-│ └────────────────────────────────────┘    │
355-│                                           │
356:│ [✓ Eligible only] [Platform▼] [Price▼] [+]│ ← Horizontal chip row
357-│                                           │
358-│ ───────── 12 campaigns ──────────────     │
359-│                                           │
--
409-└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
410-```
411-
412:**States:** Eligible (full CTA), Ineligible (disabled, first failing row auto-scrolled + pulsed), Already-applied (status chip), Already-approved (CTA becomes "Submit your video"), Now-inactive (banner + CTA hidden).
413-
414-**Inline `AddHandleSheet` flow (spec).** Tapping "Add your IG handle" (or "Add your TikTok handle") on any failing EligibilityRow opens a bottom sheet without leaving Campaign Detail. This keeps the creator's intent intact — they came here to apply to *this* campaign, not to manage their profile.
415-
--
628-
629-- **Notification copy:** "@cleanco updated Clean Beauty Review. Your application was cancelled — you can re-apply with the new terms."
630-- **Deep link:** Application Detail with a distinctive **"What changed" diff card** at top (cancelled-soft bg, cancelled-color 2px border, no shadow).
631:- **Diff card:** side-by-side old vs new for changed fields (price, pre/post-conditions). Changed values in primary-soft pills.
632-- **Primary CTA:** "Re-apply" (cta color) — takes creator to new campaign detail pre-scrolled to eligibility.
633-- **Secondary:** "Dismiss".
634:- **Motion:** status pill transitions pending/approved → cancelled with 600ms color wash (no bounce — neutral).
635-- **Lister side:** "This will cancel **N** pending applications. They'll be notified." Requires explicit confirmation button.
636-[Omitted long context line]
637-
--
641-
642-**Approved:**
643-- Deep-link to Submission Detail.
644:- StatusPill: 200ms warning → 320ms color-interpolated success + bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1).
645-- Confetti-lite: 8 small 6×6 squares burst upward, fade over 800ms.
646-- Haptic: `Success`.
647-- Banner: "Approved — nice work!" cta-colored, auto-dismiss 2.5s.
--
649-
650-**Rejected:**
651-- Same deep-link.
652:- StatusPill: 400ms warning → danger wash + **one shake gesture made of 3 oscillation cycles** (±4px each, 120ms per half-cycle, ~720ms total). No confetti.
653-- Haptic: `Warning` (not Error — not punitive).
654-- Lister feedback card always visible (danger-soft bg).
655-- **Primary CTA: "Find a similar bounty"** — routes to Feed pre-filtered by the rejected campaign's `listings.category` + a price band of ±30% around its `listings.price_cents`. Both columns exist in the schema (see `tech-architecture.md` §7); the feed uses the `listings_category_price_idx` partial index for sub-ms response. The rejected path must offer forward motion, not just a closed door.
--
665-
666-| State | Visual | When |
667-|---|---|---|
668:| `fresh` | no chip; timestamp reads "Updated 2m ago" | last snapshot for this `scrape_mode` is `fresh` and `< 24h` old |
669-| `stale` | tiny warning-soft dot; timestamp reads "Updated 3d ago" | `fresh` but `> 24h` (auto-flipped by cron) |
670-| `refreshing` | faint pulsing skeleton over the metric value | a webhook is outstanding |
671:| `failed` | danger-soft chip: "Couldn't refresh · Retry" | last snapshot is `failed`; tapping retries (respects 6h throttle) |
672-
673-**Pull-to-refresh gesture** on Profile:
674-- **Pull phase:** counter above metric tiles interpolates upward; "Release to refresh".
675-- **Release phase:** all per-metric tiles owned by the platform(s) being refreshed enter `refreshing` simultaneously. Per-platform odometer only starts when that mode's webhook lands.
676:- **Partial-success phase (IG):** if `ig_details` lands first, follower count runs the odometer + delta pill; `ig_avg_views_last_10` stays in `refreshing` with its skeleton until `ig_posts` lands. If `ig_posts` fails (e.g. photo-only profile), the views tile flips to `failed` with "Not enough video content" as the specific copy (we distinguish "no videos" from "scrape error" by checking `raw_payload`).
677:- **Full-success phase:** each mode's tile runs its own odometer with bouncy spring and fades its delta pill (`+1.2K`/`-300`) for 1.5s.
678-- **Error phase:** if a manual refresh hits the 6h throttle (`429 RATE_LIMIT`), toast surfaces the exact `retry_after_sec` using a **tiered format**:
679-  - `≥ 1h`: `"Try again in Xh Ym."` (e.g. "Try again in 4h 23m.")
680-  - `60s ≤ t < 1h`: `"Try again in Xm."` (e.g. "Try again in 7m.")
--
712-| Component | Purpose | Key props |
713-|---|---|---|
714-| **CampaignCard** | Feed + inbox cell | `title, company, platforms[], price, eligibility, variant: 'creator'\|'lister', counts?` |
715:| **StatusPill** | Status indicator | `status`, exposes `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet |
716-| **EligibilityBadge** | Compact badge on card | `eligible: boolean, reason?` |
717-| **EligibilityRow** | Single pre-condition row | `label, required, user_value, passed, action?` |
718-| **ConditionChecklist** | Post-condition list | `items[], mode: 'self'\|'review', onChange` |
719:| **MetricChip** | Follower / avg-views chip | `platform, label, value, delta?, lastUpdated?` |
720-| **VideoURLInput** | TikTok/IG URL with preview | `platform, onChange, onPreview` |
721-| **VideoThumbnail** | 9:16 or 1:1 preview | `url, aspect, onPress` |
722-| **ButtonPrimary** | CTA button | `label, onPress, loading, disabled, variant` |
723-| **ButtonSecondary** | Low-emphasis button | `label, onPress, loading, disabled` |
724-| **FAB** | Floating action (Lister) | `icon, onPress` |
725:| **Chip** | Filter + category | `label, active, variant, onPress` |
726-| **SectionHeader** | ══════ Label ══════ | `title, action?` |
727-| **ProgressDots** | Wizard progress | `total, current` |
728-| **DiffCard** | "What changed" card | `oldValue, newValue, fieldName` |
--
736-| **CelebrationBurst** | Approval confetti | `anchorRef` |
737-| **TabBar** | Custom tab bar per role | `tabs[], activeIndex` |
738-| **NotificationRow** | Inbox item | `type, title, timestamp, read, href` |
739:| **ReuseBadge** | "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip on submission row / review sheet — surfaces cross-posting without blocking it | `count, variant: 'inline'\|'header'` |
740-| **OverrideEligibilityDialog** | Approve-time confirmation for listers overriding a failed post-condition; renders the failed-conditions summary + required typed confirmation | `failedConditions[], onConfirm, onCancel` |
741:| **MetricStaleIndicator** | Per-metric tri-state marker attached to `MetricChip` (fresh / stale / refreshing / failed) — drives the visual in §5.4 | `state: 'fresh'\|'stale'\|'refreshing'\|'failed', retryAfterSec?, errorKind?: 'no_videos'\|'scrape_error', onRetry?` |
742-| **AddHandleSheet** | Bottom-sheet flow that adds a TikTok/IG handle inline from any surface (Campaign Detail eligibility row, Edit Handles). Owns its own state machine across `input → dispatching → scraping → (partial) → fresh \| failed` — see §4.2 | `platform: 'tiktok'\|'instagram', preFilledHandle?: string, onSuccess: (socialLinkId) => void, onCancel: () => void` |
743-
744----
--
766-- [ ] Press/hover transitions 150–300ms
767-- [ ] Body text contrast verified ≥ 4.5:1 (`#0F172A` on `#FFF9F2` = 17.8:1, AAA)
768-- [ ] Primary pink `#EC4899` never used as foreground text at body size (fails AAA at 3.3:1) — swap to `--primary-deep` `#831843` where text meaning is required
769:- [ ] All content text respects system Dynamic Type / font scale up to 130% (`maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`); UI chrome (pills, tab labels, status chips) opts out with `allowFontScaling={false}`
770-- [ ] All `EmptyState` usages reference a key from the illustration registry; no loose illustration SVGs
771-- [ ] Focus states visible for external keyboard
772-- [ ] `useReducedMotion()` respected on all spring/shake/confetti

644:- StatusPill: 200ms warning → 320ms color-interpolated success + bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1).
645-- Confetti-lite: 8 small 6×6 squares burst upward, fade over 800ms.
646-- Haptic: `Success`.
647-- Banner: "Approved — nice work!" cta-colored, auto-dismiss 2.5s.
--
649-
650-**Rejected:**
651-- Same deep-link.
652:- StatusPill: 400ms warning → danger wash + **one shake gesture made of 3 oscillation cycles** (±4px each, 120ms per half-cycle, ~720ms total). No confetti.
653-- Haptic: `Warning` (not Error — not punitive).
654-- Lister feedback card always visible (danger-soft bg).
655-- **Primary CTA: "Find a similar bounty"** — routes to Feed pre-filtered by the rejected campaign's `listings.category` + a price band of ±30% around its `listings.price_cents`. Both columns exist in the schema (see `tech-architecture.md` §7); the feed uses the `listings_category_price_idx` partial index for sub-ms response. The rejected path must offer forward motion, not just a closed door.
--
665-
666-| State | Visual | When |
667-|---|---|---|
668:| `fresh` | no chip; timestamp reads "Updated 2m ago" | last snapshot for this `scrape_mode` is `fresh` and `< 24h` old |
669-| `stale` | tiny warning-soft dot; timestamp reads "Updated 3d ago" | `fresh` but `> 24h` (auto-flipped by cron) |
670-| `refreshing` | faint pulsing skeleton over the metric value | a webhook is outstanding |
671:| `failed` | danger-soft chip: "Couldn't refresh · Retry" | last snapshot is `failed`; tapping retries (respects 6h throttle) |
672-
673-**Pull-to-refresh gesture** on Profile:
674-- **Pull phase:** counter above metric tiles interpolates upward; "Release to refresh".
675-- **Release phase:** all per-metric tiles owned by the platform(s) being refreshed enter `refreshing` simultaneously. Per-platform odometer only starts when that mode's webhook lands.
676:- **Partial-success phase (IG):** if `ig_details` lands first, follower count runs the odometer + delta pill; `ig_avg_views_last_10` stays in `refreshing` with its skeleton until `ig_posts` lands. If `ig_posts` fails (e.g. photo-only profile), the views tile flips to `failed` with "Not enough video content" as the specific copy (we distinguish "no videos" from "scrape error" by checking `raw_payload`).
677:- **Full-success phase:** each mode's tile runs its own odometer with bouncy spring and fades its delta pill (`+1.2K`/`-300`) for 1.5s.
678-- **Error phase:** if a manual refresh hits the 6h throttle (`429 RATE_LIMIT`), toast surfaces the exact `retry_after_sec` using a **tiered format**:
679-  - `≥ 1h`: `"Try again in Xh Ym."` (e.g. "Try again in 4h 23m.")
680-  - `60s ≤ t < 1h`: `"Try again in Xm."` (e.g. "Try again in 7m.")
--

input.prompt:
Review the US-026 implementation for the Marketify project. This is a Ralph autonomous-loop iteration and I need an independent pass before commit.

## Story (US-026: Primitive Chip + StatusPill)
Acceptance criteria:
1. `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx` + `StatusPill.tsx`
2. Chip is interactive (selectable); StatusPill is read-only with semantic color from prop
3. Mobile-mcp verification screen: render all four status pills + chips in selected/unselected
4. Commit

## Diff scope
Only review these files:
- `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/primitives/StatusPill.tsx` (new)
- `src/design/typography.ts` (added `micro` textStyle — Rubik_700Bold, 11pt, lineHeight 14, letterSpacing 0.88, textTransform uppercase)
- `app/index.tsx` (extended preview screen with chip row + pill row — replaced the button-only layout with a ScrollView)

## Relevant spec sections
- `docs/design.md` §1.2 (colors — primarySoft is "selected chip bg", soft-tinted bgs for status pills)
- `docs/design.md` §1.3 line 71 (Micro = Satoshi 700 @ 11, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.08em — we're substituting Rubik_700Bold per the US-023 font fallback chain)
- `docs/design.md` §1.4 line 100 (press = spring.soft scale 0.97)
- `docs/design.md` §1.5 lines 116-118 (pill=999 radius, 2px border, hard shadow)
- `docs/design.md` §7 line 715 (StatusPill props: `status`, `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet)
- `docs/design.md` §7 line 725 (Chip props: `label, active, variant, onPress`)

## Known scope deferrals (do NOT flag these as issues)
- Chip `variant` prop is intentionally omitted. The spec lists `variant` but doesn't define its values; no v1 caller needs category chips yet. US-025 (ButtonPrimary variant) set the precedent for deferring undefined variant props. I'll note as spec gap in progress.
- StatusPill `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet is intentionally deferred to US-060 (approval/rejection animations) — this story is "read-only with semantic color" per AC.
- The app/index.tsx preview screen is the existing dev surface — no routing wiring yet (that's US-031).

## Codebase conventions to check against
- Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (Codebase Patterns #78): shared `pressed: 0→1` value driving `useAnimatedStyle`, guard handlePressIn/Out with `if (!interactive) return;`, Pressable `disabled={!interactive}`.
- `withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced)` inline (not cached) — `reduced` can flip at runtime (Codebase Patterns #76).
- No emoji in product UI (Lucide icons only per design.md).
- `allowFontScaling={false}` on UI chrome pills/chips (design.md §8 checklist line 769).
- TypeScript strict — no `any` without eslint-disable + comment.

Focus on: correctness, accessibility, design-spec fidelity, consistency with existing Button primitive.

Report only medium-or-higher issues. Low/nit items can be listed separately but I'll only fix medium+.

content.text:
I now have everything I need to conduct a thorough review. Let me compile the findings.

---

Reviewing US-026: `Chip.tsx`, `StatusPill.tsx`, `src/design/typography.ts`, and `app/index.tsx` against the project spec, codebase patterns, and the existing Button primitive.

---

## Critical Issues

### 1. Chip is missing the hard shadow — violates §1.5 "lifted" Neubrutalist contract (Confidence: 92)

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx`

`Button.tsx` applies `shadows.hard` to its `Animated.View` (via `styles.base` + the `shadows.hard` spread) and collapses it to `(0,0)` on press by animating `shadowOpacity` from 1 to 0 alongside `translateX/Y(3,3)`. The §1.5 elevation table says two tiers: "flat" and "lifted (hard shadow)." A chip sits in the "lifted" tier the same way buttons do.

The Button primitive also collapses the shadow by animating `translateX/Y` alongside `shadowOpacity`. The Chip's `animatedStyle` only touches `scale` — if a shadow were added later it still wouldn't collapse correctly on press.

**Fix:** Import `shadows` from `@/design/tokens`, add it to the non-disabled chip style, and update `animatedStyle` to match the Button pattern:

```ts
const animatedStyle = useAnimatedStyle(() => {
  const v = pressed.value;
  return {
    transform: [{ translateX: v * 3 }, { translateY: v * 3 }, { scale: 1 - v * 0.03 }],
    shadowOpacity: 1 - v,

});
```

Then in the `Animated.View` style array, add `disabled ? shadows.none : shadows.hard` before `animatedStyle`.

### 2. StatusPill is missing the hard shadow — violates §1.5 explicit "pills" rule (Confidence: 88)

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/StatusPill.tsx`

§1.5 lines 116–118 (as cited in the review brief): "pill = 999 radius, 2px border, hard shadow." The `StatusPill.styles.base` (lines 43–51) has `borderRadius: radii.pill` and `borderWidth: 2` but no shadow properties. The `shadows.hard` token is never imported.

**Fix:** Import `shadows` and spread it into `styles.base`, or apply it inline on the `View` style array. Since the pill is read-only (not animated), a simple spread suffices:

```ts
const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  base: {
    alignSelf: 'flex-start',
    paddingHorizontal: spacing.md,
    paddingVertical: 4,
    borderRadius: radii.pill,
    borderWidth: 2,
    ...shadows.hard,
  },
});
```

---

### 3. Chip `accessibilityRole` should be `"checkbox"` or `"switch"`, not `"button"` (Confidence: 85)

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx`, line 73

The Chip is a toggle/filter (selected / unselected state conveyed via `accessibilityState={{ selected: active }}`). In ARIA semantics `role="button"` ignores the `aria-selected` attribute — screen readers will read the element as a button, not as a selectable item, and the `selected` state will be silently swallowed on most platforms. The correct role for a selectable filter chip is `"checkbox"` (maps to `aria-checked`) or the React Native `"togglebutton"` role (RN 0.73+). Using `"button"` with `selected` is incoherent to assistive technology.

Design spec §8 checklist: "All Pressable components have press feedback" and implicit accessibility throughout. The pattern review brief calls out "Neubrutalist Pressable pattern #78" which implies the same accessibility care the Button takes.

**Fix:** Use `accessibilityRole="checkbox"` and replace `accessibilityState={{ selected: active, disabled: !interactive }}` with `accessibilityState={{ checked: active, disabled: !interactive }}`, which is the semantically consistent pair for `"checkbox"`.

---

### 4. StatusPill violates design §7 anti-pattern: "color is never the sole status indicator" (Confidence: 83)

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/StatusPill.tsx`

Design spec §7 anti-patterns (line 775 of the checklist): "Color is never the sole status indicator." The spec anti-pattern list and §1.5 iconography note both state every status uses "color + icon + text." This is a partial violation for the MVP — the text label is present, so color is not the _sole_ indicator, but there is no icon. This likely belongs in the US-060 scope for full compliance, however the `accessibilityRole="text"` is also wrong for a `View`. `View` doesn't accept `accessibilityRole="text"` in RN — the role should be omitted or set to `"none"`, and the `Text` child carries the semantic meaning already via `accessibilityLabel` on its parent.

**Fix (unambiguous part):** Remove `accessibilityRole="text"` from the `View` (it is not a valid `accessibilityRole` value for a `View` in React Native; valid roles are `"none"`, `"button"`, `"image"`, etc.). The `accessibilityLabel` on the `View` is sufficient. This is the actionable fix for this story; the icon-per-status requirement can be deferred to US-060.

**File:** `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx`, lines 52–56

This is actually correct — `Haptics.selectionAsync()` is called _after_ the `if (!interactive) return` guard. Ignoring this one — it passes review.

The `textTransform` value `'uppercase'` narrows correctly because of the `satisfies Record<string, TextStyle>` constraint. This is fine — TypeScript will enforce the correct union. No issue here.

## Summary

Two missing hard shadows (Chip and StatusPill) are the primary structural bugs — both violate an explicit §1.5 rule that the existing Button primitive correctly implements. The `accessibilityRole="button"` on a toggle chip incorrectly signals semantics to assistive technology. The `accessibilityRole="text"` on the StatusPill `View` is an invalid RN value that should be removed.

- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/primitives/StatusPill.tsx` — add hard shadow + remove invalid `accessibilityRole` from the View

toolUseResult.prompt:
Review the US-026 implementation for the Marketify project. This is a Ralph autonomous-loop iteration and I need an independent pass before commit.

## Story (US-026: Primitive Chip + StatusPill)
Acceptance criteria:
1. `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx` + `StatusPill.tsx`
2. Chip is interactive (selectable); StatusPill is read-only with semantic color from prop
3. Mobile-mcp verification screen: render all four status pills + chips in selected/unselected
4. Commit

## Diff scope
Only review these files:
- `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/primitives/StatusPill.tsx` (new)
- `src/design/typography.ts` (added `micro` textStyle — Rubik_700Bold, 11pt, lineHeight 14, letterSpacing 0.88, textTransform uppercase)
- `app/index.tsx` (extended preview screen with chip row + pill row — replaced the button-only layout with a ScrollView)

## Relevant spec sections
- `docs/design.md` §1.2 (colors — primarySoft is "selected chip bg", soft-tinted bgs for status pills)
- `docs/design.md` §1.3 line 71 (Micro = Satoshi 700 @ 11, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.08em — we're substituting Rubik_700Bold per the US-023 font fallback chain)
- `docs/design.md` §1.4 line 100 (press = spring.soft scale 0.97)
- `docs/design.md` §1.5 lines 116-118 (pill=999 radius, 2px border, hard shadow)
- `docs/design.md` §7 line 715 (StatusPill props: `status`, `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet)
- `docs/design.md` §7 line 725 (Chip props: `label, active, variant, onPress`)

## Known scope deferrals (do NOT flag these as issues)
- Chip `variant` prop is intentionally omitted. The spec lists `variant` but doesn't define its values; no v1 caller needs category chips yet. US-025 (ButtonPrimary variant) set the precedent for deferring undefined variant props. I'll note as spec gap in progress.
- StatusPill `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet is intentionally deferred to US-060 (approval/rejection animations) — this story is "read-only with semantic color" per AC.
- The app/index.tsx preview screen is the existing dev surface — no routing wiring yet (that's US-031).

## Codebase conventions to check against
- Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (Codebase Patterns #78): shared `pressed: 0→1` value driving `useAnimatedStyle`, guard handlePressIn/Out with `if (!interactive) return;`, Pressable `disabled={!interactive}`.
- `withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced)` inline (not cached) — `reduced` can flip at runtime (Codebase Patterns #76).
- No emoji in product UI (Lucide icons only per design.md).
- `allowFontScaling={false}` on UI chrome pills/chips (design.md §8 checklist line 769).
- TypeScript strict — no `any` without eslint-disable + comment.

Focus on: correctness, accessibility, design-spec fidelity, consistency with existing Button primitive.

Report only medium-or-higher issues. Low/nit items can be listed separately but I'll only fix medium+.

content.content:
75	- **Apify transitional run statuses are READY, RUNNING, TIMING-OUT, ABORTING.** When `waitForFinish` expires before a run reaches a terminal state, Apify returns one of these four — in all four cases the run continues and the registered webhook will still fire the terminal event later. Map all four to 'refreshing' in any caller that derives a metrics-status from the immediate actor-run response; only `SUCCEEDED` should map to 'fresh' and any other (FAILED/TIMED-OUT/ABORTED) to 'failed'. Don't special-case only RUNNING.
76	- **Reanimated reduced-motion pattern (callers + helpers):** `withReducedMotion(config, reduced)` in `src/design/motion.ts` spreads `reduceMotion: ReduceMotion.Always` onto any Reanimated `SpringConfig`/`TimingConfig` when the caller's `useReducedMotion()` is true. Reanimated v4 collapses `Always`-flagged animations to an instant snap on `onStart` — this is the sanctioned no-op; do NOT roll your own `withTiming(target, {duration: 0})` wrapper. Call shape: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))` — the **explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic is mandatory** because the presets are declared `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` which narrows them to readonly literal types; under `strict` TS those literals don't satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint without widening. Inline the helper inside `withSpring` rather than caching to a `const spring` — `reduced` can flip at runtime when the user toggles OS reduce-motion. `useReducedMotion()` in `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` uses a module-scope `cached: boolean | null` so only the first hook mount per app session pays the async `AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled()` race — subsequent mounts read the cached value synchronously and never flash `false` on devices with reduce-motion on.
77	- **Animatable iOS shadow props in `useAnimatedStyle`: `shadowOpacity` + `shadowRadius` only — NOT `shadowOffset`.** `shadowOffset` is a compound `{width, height}` object prop that Reanimated 4 does not interpolate through `useAnimatedStyle`; the underlying UIKit shadow stays at its `StyleSheet` value and the offset never moves. For the neubrutalist "shadow collapses to (0,0) on press" spec (docs/design.md §1.5 line 118), the sanctioned pattern is to **animate `shadowOpacity` from 1 → 0** while simultaneously translating the view `(0,0) → (3,3)` — the shadow visually disappears into the pressed-down button. Reference: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`.
78	- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.
79	
80	---
81

84	- Created `src/{components,screens,lib,types}/` each with `.gitkeep`; added `src/index.ts` stub (`export {};`) to satisfy `expo lint`.

file.content:
- **Apify transitional run statuses are READY, RUNNING, TIMING-OUT, ABORTING.** When `waitForFinish` expires before a run reaches a terminal state, Apify returns one of these four — in all four cases the run continues and the registered webhook will still fire the terminal event later. Map all four to 'refreshing' in any caller that derives a metrics-status from the immediate actor-run response; only `SUCCEEDED` should map to 'fresh' and any other (FAILED/TIMED-OUT/ABORTED) to 'failed'. Don't special-case only RUNNING.
- **Reanimated reduced-motion pattern (callers + helpers):** `withReducedMotion(config, reduced)` in `src/design/motion.ts` spreads `reduceMotion: ReduceMotion.Always` onto any Reanimated `SpringConfig`/`TimingConfig` when the caller's `useReducedMotion()` is true. Reanimated v4 collapses `Always`-flagged animations to an instant snap on `onStart` — this is the sanctioned no-op; do NOT roll your own `withTiming(target, {duration: 0})` wrapper. Call shape: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))` — the **explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic is mandatory** because the presets are declared `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` which narrows them to readonly literal types; under `strict` TS those literals don't satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint without widening. Inline the helper inside `withSpring` rather than caching to a `const spring` — `reduced` can flip at runtime when the user toggles OS reduce-motion. `useReducedMotion()` in `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` uses a module-scope `cached: boolean | null` so only the first hook mount per app session pays the async `AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled()` race — subsequent mounts read the cached value synchronously and never flash `false` on devices with reduce-motion on.
- **Animatable iOS shadow props in `useAnimatedStyle`: `shadowOpacity` + `shadowRadius` only — NOT `shadowOffset`.** `shadowOffset` is a compound `{width, height}` object prop that Reanimated 4 does not interpolate through `useAnimatedStyle`; the underlying UIKit shadow stays at its `StyleSheet` value and the offset never moves. For the neubrutalist "shadow collapses to (0,0) on press" spec (docs/design.md §1.5 line 118), the sanctioned pattern is to **animate `shadowOpacity` from 1 → 0** while simultaneously translating the view `(0,0) → (3,3)` — the shadow visually disappears into the pressed-down button. Reference: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`.
- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.

---

- Created `src/{components,screens,lib,types}/` each with `.gitkeep`; added `src/index.ts` stub (`export {};`) to satisfy `expo lint`.

input.old_string:
- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.

---
input.new_string:
- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.
- **Toggle-chip accessibility: `accessibilityRole="checkbox"` with `accessibilityState={{ checked }}`, NOT `"button"` with `{ selected }`.** `role="button"` ignores `aria-selected` — most screen readers silently drop it, so a filter chip reads as an inert button and the user can't tell what's on vs off. `checkbox` maps cleanly to `aria-checked` on iOS/Android and the `checked` boolean surfaces as "checked"/"not checked" in VoiceOver/TalkBack. Applies to any toggleable pill (filter chips, segmented controls, multi-select tags). Reference: `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx`. For read-only `View`s (e.g. StatusPill), do NOT set `accessibilityRole="text"` — that role is for `Text` nodes only and iOS silently rejects it on a `View`; instead use `accessible` + `accessibilityLabel` on the `View` and let the child `Text` inherit silently.
- **`textStyles.micro` is the single source of truth for pill/chip labels** — Rubik_700Bold (stand-in for Satoshi 700 per the US-023 fallback chain), 11pt, lineHeight 14, `letterSpacing: 0.88`, `textTransform: 'uppercase'`. The 0.88 is the absolute-pt conversion of §1.3's `0.08em at 11pt` (RN letterSpacing is absolute points, not em). Every pill/chip/status-badge should apply `textStyles.micro` plus its semantic color inline, plus `allowFontScaling={false}` per the §8 checklist rule that UI chrome opts out of Dynamic Type. Added in US-026 (the first story that needed it); US-023 intentionally deferred micro since its AC enumerated only 6 keys.
- **Neubrutalist "lifted" tier is universal** — cards, buttons, chips, inputs, AND status pills all get `shadows.hard` from `src/design/tokens.ts` (offset 3,3, solid ink, opacity 1, radius 0). The spec's "flat" tier (§1.5 line 119) is reserved for content inside cards (e.g. EmptyState illustrations, dividers) — not standalone surfaces. Interactive lifted surfaces collapse the shadow on press via the animated `shadowOpacity` pattern; read-only lifted surfaces (StatusPill) just spread `shadows.hard` into their static style. Don't skip the shadow on small pills "because they're small" — it's what makes the status pills visually readable at a glance in a dense inbox row.

---

Call shape: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))` — the **explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic is mandatory** because the presets are declared `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` which narrows them to readonly literal types; under `strict` TS those literals don't satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint without widening. Inline the helper inside `withSpring` rather than caching to a `const spring` — `reduced` can flip at runtime when the user toggles OS reduce-motion. `useReducedMotion()` in `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` uses a module-scope `cached: boolean | null` so only the first hook mount per app session pays the async `AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled()` race — subsequent mounts read the cached value synchronously and never flash `false` on devices with reduce-motion on.

- **Animatable iOS shadow props in `useAnimatedStyle`: `shadowOpacity` + `shadowRadius` only — NOT `shadowOffset`.** `shadowOffset` is a compound `{width, height}` object prop that Reanimated 4 does not interpolate through `useAnimatedStyle`; the underlying UIKit shadow stays at its `StyleSheet` value and the offset never moves. For the neubrutalist "shadow collapses to (0,0) on press" spec (docs/design.md §1.5 line 118), the sanctioned pattern is to **animate `shadowOpacity` from 1 → 0** while simultaneously translating the view `(0,0) → (3,3)` — the shadow visually disappears into the pressed-down button. Reference: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`.

- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.

+- **Toggle-chip accessibility: `accessibilityRole="checkbox"` with `accessibilityState={{ checked }}`, NOT `"button"` with `{ selected }`.** `role="button"` ignores `aria-selected` — most screen readers silently drop it, so a filter chip reads as an inert button and the user can't tell what's on vs off. `checkbox` maps cleanly to `aria-checked` on iOS/Android and the `checked` boolean surfaces as "checked"/"not checked" in VoiceOver/TalkBack. Applies to any toggleable pill (filter chips, segmented controls, multi-select tags). Reference: `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx`. For read-only `View`s (e.g. StatusPill), do NOT set `accessibilityRole="text"` — that role is for `Text` nodes only and iOS silently rejects it on a `View`; instead use `accessible` + `accessibilityLabel` on the `View` and let the child `Text` inherit silently.

+- **`textStyles.micro` is the single source of truth for pill/chip labels** — Rubik_700Bold (stand-in for Satoshi 700 per the US-023 fallback chain), 11pt, lineHeight 14, `letterSpacing: 0.88`, `textTransform: 'uppercase'`. The 0.88 is the absolute-pt conversion of §1.3's `0.08em at 11pt` (RN letterSpacing is absolute points, not em). Every pill/chip/status-badge should apply `textStyles.micro` plus its semantic color inline, plus `allowFontScaling={false}` per the §8 checklist rule that UI chrome opts out of Dynamic Type. Added in US-026 (the first story that needed it); US-023 intentionally deferred micro since its AC enumerated only 6 keys.

+- **Neubrutalist "lifted" tier is universal** — cards, buttons, chips, inputs, AND status pills all get `shadows.hard` from `src/design/tokens.ts` (offset 3,3, solid ink, opacity 1, radius 0). The spec's "flat" tier (§1.5 line 119) is reserved for content inside cards (e.g. EmptyState illustrations, dividers) — not standalone surfaces. Interactive lifted surfaces collapse the shadow on press via the animated `shadowOpacity` pattern; read-only lifted surfaces (StatusPill) just spread `shadows.hard` into their static style. Don't skip the shadow on small pills "because they're small" — it's what makes the status pills visually readable at a glance in a dense inbox row.
